141	class SglFunction:
142	    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
143	        self.func = func
144	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
145	        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
146	        self.pin_prefix_rid = None
147	
148	        # Parse arguments
149	        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
150	        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'
151	        self.arg_names = argspec.args[1:]
152	        self.arg_defaults = argspec.defaults if argspec.defaults is not None else []
153	
154	    def bind(self, **kwargs):
155	        assert all(key in self.arg_names for key in kwargs)
156	
157	        new_bind_dict = {**self.bind_arguments, **kwargs}
158	        return SglFunction(self.func, bind_arguments=new_bind_dict)
159	
160	    def run(
161	        self,
162	        *args,
163	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
164	        n: int = 1,
165	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
166	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
167	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
168	        temperature: float = 1.0,
169	        top_p: float = 1.0,
170	        top_k: int = -1,
171	        min_p: float = 0.0,
172	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
173	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
174	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
175	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
176	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
177	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
178	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
179	        stream: bool = False,
180	        backend=None,
181	        use_thread: bool = True,
182	        **kwargs,
183	    ):
184	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program
185	
186	        # avoid using [] as the default arg: https://nikos7am.com/posts/mutable-default-arguments/
187	        if stop is None:
188	            stop = []
189	        if stop_token_ids is None:
190	            stop_token_ids = []
191	        if stop_regex is None:
192	            stop_regex = []
193	
194	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
195	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
196	            n=n,
197	            stop=stop,
198	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
199	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
200	            temperature=temperature,
201	            top_p=top_p,
202	            top_k=top_k,
203	            min_p=min_p,
204	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
205	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
206	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
207	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
208	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
209	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
210	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
211	        )
212	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
213	        return run_program(
214	            self,
215	            backend,
216	            args,
217	            kwargs,
218	            default_sampling_para,
219	            stream,
220	            use_thread=use_thread,
221	        )
222	
223	    def run_batch(
224	        self,
225	        batch_kwargs,
226	        *,
227	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
228	        n: int = 1,
229	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
230	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
231	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
232	        temperature: float = 1.0,
233	        top_p: float = 1.0,
234	        top_k: int = -1,
235	        min_p: float = 0.0,
236	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
237	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
238	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
239	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
240	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
241	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
242	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
243	        backend=None,
244	        num_threads: Union[str, int] = "auto",
245	        progress_bar: bool = False,
246	        generator_style: bool = False,
247	    ):
248	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program_batch
249	
250	        if stop is None:
251	            stop = []
252	        if stop_token_ids is None:
253	            stop_token_ids = []
254	        if stop_regex is None:
255	            stop_regex = []
256	
257	        assert isinstance(batch_kwargs, (list, tuple))
258	        if len(batch_kwargs) == 0:
259	            return []
260	        if not isinstance(batch_kwargs[0], dict):
261	            num_programs = len(batch_kwargs)
262	            # change the list of argument values to dict of arg_name -> arg_value
263	            batch_kwargs = [
264	                {self.arg_names[i]: v for i, v in enumerate(arg_values)}
265	                for arg_values in batch_kwargs
266	                if isinstance(arg_values, (list, tuple))
267	                and len(self.arg_names) - len(self.arg_defaults)
268	                <= len(arg_values)
269	                <= len(self.arg_names)
270	            ]
271	            # Ensure to raise an exception if the number of arguments mismatch
272	            if len(batch_kwargs) != num_programs:
273	                raise Exception("Given arguments mismatch the SGL function signature")
274	
275	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
276	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
277	            n=n,
278	            stop=stop,
279	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
280	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
281	            temperature=temperature,
282	            top_p=top_p,
283	            top_k=top_k,
284	            min_p=min_p,
285	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
286	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
287	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
288	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
289	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
290	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
291	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
292	        )
293	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
294	        return run_program_batch(
295	            self,
296	            backend,
297	            batch_kwargs,
298	            default_sampling_para,
299	            num_threads,
300	            progress_bar,
301	            generator_style=generator_style,
302	        )
303	
304	    def trace(self, *, backend=None, **kwargs):
305	        from sglang.lang.tracer import trace_program
306	
307	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
308	        return trace_program(self, kwargs, backend)
309	
310	    def cache(self, backend=None):
311	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import cache_program
312	
313	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
314	        return cache_program(self, backend)
315	
316	    def __call__(self, *args, **kwargs):
317	        from sglang.lang.tracer import TracingScope
318	
319	        tracing_scope = TracingScope.get_current_scope()
320	        if tracing_scope is None:
321	            return self.run(*args, **kwargs)
322	        else:
323	            kwargs["backend"] = tracing_scope.tracer_state.backend
324	            return self.trace(*args, **kwargs)

327	class SglExpr:
328	    node_ct = 0
329	
330	    def __init__(self):
331	        self.node_id = SglExpr.node_ct
332	        self.prev_node = None
333	        self.pid = None
334	        SglExpr.node_ct += 1
335	
336	    def __add__(self, other):
337	        if isinstance(other, str):
338	            other = SglConstantText(other)
339	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr)
340	
341	        return self.concatenate_ir(self, other)
342	
343	    def __radd__(self, other):
344	        if isinstance(other, str):
345	            other = SglConstantText(other)
346	        assert isinstance(other, SglExpr), f"{other}"
347	
348	        return self.concatenate_ir(other, self)
349	
350	    def concatenate_ir(self, a, b):
351	        if isinstance(a, SglExprList):
352	            if isinstance(b, SglExprList):
353	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + b.expr_list)
354	            else:
355	                return SglExprList(a.expr_list + [b])
356	        elif isinstance(b, SglExprList):
357	            return SglExprList([a] + b.expr_list)
358	
359	        return SglExprList([a, b])
360	
361	    def print_graph_dfs(self):
362	        ret = [""]
363	        visited = set()
364	
365	        def dfs_print(x):
366	            if x is None or x in visited:
367	                return
368	            visited.add(x)
369	
370	            # Print dependency
371	            if x.prev_node is not None:
372	                dfs_print(x.prev_node)
373	
374	            if isinstance(x, SglExprList):
375	                for y in x.expr_list:
376	                    dfs_print(y)
377	            # elif isinstance(x, SglRole):
378	            #    dfs_print(x.expr)
379	            elif isinstance(x, SglVariable):
380	                dfs_print(x.source)
381	
382	            # Print the node itself
383	            if isinstance(x, (SglFork, SglGetForkItem)):
384	                ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = {x}\n"
385	            else:
386	                if x.prev_node is not None:
387	                    ret[0] += (
388	                        f"%{x.node_id} = %{x.prev_node.node_id} + " + str(x) + "\n"
389	                    )
390	                else:
391	                    ret[0] += f"%{x.node_id} = " + str(x) + "\n"
392	
393	        dfs_print(self)
394	        return ret[0]
395

619	class SglSeparateReasoning(SglExpr):
620	    def __init__(self, model_type: str, expr: SglExpr):
621	        super().__init__()
622	        self.model_type = model_type
623	
624	        self.expr = expr
625	        self.name = None
626	        self._process_expr(expr)
627	
628	    def process_name_for_reasoning(self, name):
629	        if not name:
630	            raise ValueError("name must be provided")
631	        return f"{name}_reasoning_content"
632	
633	    def _process_expr(self, expr):
634	        if isinstance(expr, SglGen):
635	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
636	        elif isinstance(expr, SglSelect):
637	            self.name = self.process_name_for_reasoning(expr.name)
638	        elif isinstance(expr, SglExprList):
639	            for x in expr.expr_list:
640	                self._process_expr(x)
641	
642	    def __repr__(self):
643	        return f"SeparateReasoning(model_type={self.model_type}, name={self.name})"

# Copyright 2023-2024 SGLang Team
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
# ==============================================================================
"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""

import logging
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Union

# sre_parse is deprecated in Python 3.11+, use re._parser instead
try:
    import re._parser as sre_parse
except ImportError:
    import sre_parse  # Python < 3.11

_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class SamplingParams:
    """
    The sampling parameters.

    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
    for the documentation.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        temperature: float = 1.0,
        top_p: float = 1.0,
        top_k: int = -1,
        min_p: float = 0.0,
        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
        n: int = 1,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
        ignore_eos: bool = False,
        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> None:
        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
        self.stop_strs = stop
        if stop_token_ids:
            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
        else:
            self.stop_token_ids = None
        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
        self.temperature = temperature
        self.top_p = top_p
        self.top_k = top_k
        self.min_p = min_p
        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
        self.regex = regex
        self.n = n
        self.json_schema = json_schema
        self.ebnf = ebnf
        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
        self.custom_params = custom_params
        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed

        # Process some special cases
        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
            self.temperature = 1.0
            self.top_k = 1
        if self.top_k == -1:
            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary

    def verify(self, vocab_size):
        if self.temperature < 0.0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
            )
        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
            raise ValueError(
                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
            )
        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
            )
        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
            )
        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
            )
        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
            raise ValueError(
                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
            )
        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
                )
            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
                )
        if self.logit_bias is not None:
            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
                    raise ValueError(
                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
                        f"{token_id}."
                    )

        grammars = [
            self.json_schema,
            self.regex,
            self.ebnf,
        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")

    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
        # Process stop strings
        if self.stop_strs is None:
            self.stop_strs = []
            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
        else:
            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]

            stop_str_max_len = 0
            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
                if tokenizer is not None:
                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
                else:
                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len

        # Process stop regex strings
        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
            self.stop_regex_strs = []
            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
        else:
            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]

            stop_regex_max_len = 0
            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
                stop_regex_max_len = max(
                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
                )

            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len


# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
# to be buffered to match the input regex string
# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
# token
def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))


MAX_LEN = 2**30


def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
    total = 0
    for token, value in subpattern:
        if token in {
            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
        }:
            total += 1
        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
            # EG: (a\d+) ->
            # [(SUBPATTERN,
            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
            _, branches = value
            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
                total += MAX_LEN
            else:
                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
            # length
            total += 0
        else:
            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")

            total += MAX_LEN

    return total

37	            self.grammar_backend = None

39	        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
40	        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
41	        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
42	        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank
43	
44	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
45	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
46	            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
47	        )
48	
49	    def __len__(self):
50	        return len(self.grammar_queue)
51	
52	    def clear(self):
53	        if self.grammar_backend:
54	            self.grammar_backend.reset()
55	
56	    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
57	        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0
58	
59	    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
60	        for req in self.grammar_queue:
61	            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
62	                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
63	                if req.grammar:
64	                    req.grammar.cancel()
65	                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")
66	
67	    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
68	        # Init grammar cache for this request
69	        add_to_grammar_queue = False
70	        if (
71	            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
72	            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
73	            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
74	            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
75	        ):
76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"

"""The baseclass of a backend for grammar-guided constrained decoding."""

import logging
import time
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from threading import Event
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple

import torch

from sglang.srt.server_args import ServerArgs

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


@dataclass
class GrammarStats:
    compilation_time: Optional[float] = None
    schema_count: Optional[int] = None
    ebnf_size: Optional[int] = None
    is_cache_hit: bool = False
    is_grammar_aborted: bool = False
    tree_traversal_time: List[float] = field(default_factory=list)
    dispatch_type: Optional[str] = None
    num_timeout: int = 0


class BaseGrammarObject:

    def __init__(self):
        self._finished = False
        self.grammar_stats = None
        self.current_token = None

    def maybe_init_reasoning(self, reasoning: bool):
        pass

    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:
        """
        Accept a token in the grammar.
        """
        raise NotImplementedError()

    def rollback(self, k: int):
        raise NotImplementedError()

    def is_terminated(self):
        return False

    def allocate_vocab_mask(
        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
    ) -> torch.Tensor:
        raise NotImplementedError()

    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
        raise NotImplementedError()

    @staticmethod
    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
        raise NotImplementedError()

    @staticmethod
    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
        raise NotImplementedError()

    def copy(self) -> "BaseGrammarObject":

Now I have a thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me also check the backend runtime_endpoint.py to understand how dtype-to-regex conversion is done, and how we could add a `json_path` constraint there.

The key insight is: **json_path** is a fundamentally different type of constraint from regex or json_schema. The existing constraint system works during generation (token-by-token), but json_path validation would be a post-generation check (validate that the complete output is valid JSON with the specified path existing).

Let me think about the design:

**Option A: Pure post-generation validation (frontend-only)**


The json_path constraint would convert the pattern to a JSON schema regex, then validate the output after generation completes. This keeps everything in the frontend layer without touching the backend grammar system.

**Option B: Convert json_path to a regex constraint**
I could translate the JSONPath pattern into a corresponding regex and pass it to the existing regex constraint system, though this conversion is complex and not straightforward.

**Option C: New constraint type with JSON schema conversion**
Convert the json_path to a JSON schema that ensures the path exists and pass it to the existing json_schema constraint system.

Looking at how constraints flow through the architecture, I see the pattern: the frontend API accepts parameters, the IR layer carries them through SglSamplingParams, then RuntimeEndpoint converts them to either regex or json_schema before the backend processes them during generation. So the practical implementation would be adding json_path support at each layer—starting with the frontend API, then the IR layer, and finally the conversion logic in RuntimeEndpoint that transforms json_path into a constraint the backend understands.

But I'm realizing the json_path constraint actually has two distinct requirements: the output must be valid JSON, and the specified path must actually exist within that JSON structure. The json_schema constraint can handle the validity part, but ensuring a specific path exists is really about validating the structure itself. When I think about what a path like `$.store.book[*].author` means as a JSON schema, it's saying the root needs a store property, and within that...

Reconsidering the structure requirements...

Actually, I think the cleanest approach is to add `json_path` as a new parameter to the frontend, then convert it to a `json_schema` in the backend using a lightweight JSONPath parser to derive what the schema should enforce. I need to map out which files to modify and what new components to create. The main changes involve adding `json_path` support to the API layer, the intermediate representation, and the runtime, plus building a new constraint handler that converts JSONPath expressions into JSON schema constraints that the existing system can use. I'm reconsidering whether this needs to be a generation-time constraint or if it could work as post-generation validation instead. Let me look at how the dtype-to-regex conversion is handled in the runtime to understand the existing pattern better.

import atexit
import json
import multiprocessing
import time
import warnings
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Union

import aiohttp
import requests

from sglang.global_config import global_config
from sglang.lang.backend.base_backend import BaseBackend
from sglang.lang.chat_template import get_chat_template, get_chat_template_by_model_path
from sglang.lang.choices import ChoicesDecision, ChoicesSamplingMethod
from sglang.lang.interpreter import StreamExecutor
from sglang.lang.ir import (
    REGEX_BOOL,
    REGEX_FLOAT,
    REGEX_INT,
    REGEX_STR,
    SglSamplingParams,
)
from sglang.utils import http_request


class RuntimeEndpoint(BaseBackend):
    def __init__(
        self,
        base_url: str,
        api_key: Optional[str] = None,
        verify: Optional[str] = None,
        chat_template_name: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.support_concate_and_append = True

        self.base_url = base_url
        self.api_key = api_key
        self.verify = verify

        res = http_request(
            self.base_url + "/get_model_info",
            api_key=self.api_key,
            verify=self.verify,
        )
        self._assert_success(res)
        self.model_info = res.json()

        if chat_template_name:
            self.chat_template = get_chat_template(chat_template_name)
        else:
            self.chat_template = get_chat_template_by_model_path(
                self.model_info["model_path"]
            )

    def get_model_name(self):
        return self.model_info["model_path"]

    def flush_cache(self):
        res = http_request(
            self.base_url + "/flush_cache",
            api_key=self.api_key,
            verify=self.verify,
            method="POST",
        )
        self._assert_success(res)

    def get_server_info(self):
        res = http_request(
            self.base_url + "/get_server_info",
            api_key=self.api_key,
            verify=self.verify,
        )
        self._assert_success(res)
        return res.json()

    def get_chat_template(self):
        return self.chat_template

    def cache_prefix(self, prefix_str: str):
        res = http_request(
            self.base_url + "/generate",
            json={"text": prefix_str, "sampling_params": {"max_new_tokens": 0}},
            api_key=self.api_key,
            verify=self.verify,
        )
        self._assert_success(res)

    def start_profile(self):
        res = http_request(
            self.base_url + "/start_profile",
            api_key=self.api_key,
            verify=self.verify,
        )
        self._assert_success(res)

    def stop_profile(self):
        res = http_request(
            self.base_url + "/stop_profile",
            api_key=self.api_key,
            verify=self.verify,
        )
        self._assert_success(res)

    def commit_lazy_operations(self, s: StreamExecutor):
        data = {"text": s.text_, "sampling_params": {"max_new_tokens": 0}}
        self._add_images(s, data)
        res = http_request(
            self.base_url + "/generate",
            json=data,
            api_key=self.api_key,
            verify=self.verify,
        )
        self._assert_success(res)

    def fill_image(self, s: StreamExecutor):
        data = {"text": s.text_, "sampling_params": {"max_new_tokens": 0}}
        self._add_images(s, data)
        res = http_request(
            self.base_url + "/generate",

69	        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]
70	        vocab_mask.fill_(1)

75	        logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))

## Implementation Strategy

The `json_path` constraint should:
1. Accept a JSONPath pattern (e.g., `"$.store.book[*].author"`)
2. Validate that generated output is valid JSON where the specified path exists

### Key Design Decisions:

**1. Where does the conversion happen?**

Looking at the existing pattern for `dtype` → `regex` conversion:
- `dtype` is stored in `SglSamplingParams` 


- `_handle_dtype_to_regex()` in `runtime_endpoint.py` converts it to `regex` before sending to the backend

For `json_path`, I'll follow the same pattern: store it in `SglSamplingParams` and convert it to a JSON schema in the backend before sending, mirroring how dtype gets converted to regex.

**2. New core module: `json_path_constraint.py`**

I'll create this module in `python/sglang/srt/constrained/` to handle the conversion from JSONPath patterns to JSON schemas, along with validation utilities.

**3. Converting JSONPath to JSON Schema**

I'm parsing the JSONPath expression step by step to build out the corresponding nested JSON schema structure. For a path like `$.store.book[*].author`, I'd extract the steps, identify array operations, and construct a schema that enforces the required object properties and array structure. This integrates seamlessly with the existing JSON schema constraint system.

**4. Frontend API**

Now I need to thread the `json_path` parameter through the API layer. I'm adding it to `gen()` in `api.py`, then updating `SglGen.__init__()` and `SglSamplingParams` in `ir.py` to accept it. I also need to update the `to_srt_kwargs()` method and make sure `_resolve_sampling_params()` in `interpreter.py` includes `json_path` in its list of parameters to merge alongside `regex` and `json_schema`.

For the runtime conversion, I'm creating a handler method that detects when `json_path` is set, converts it to a JSON schema using a utility function, and sets that as the `json_schema` parameter while warning if both are specified.

For the initial implementation, I'm planning to support basic JSONPath features like the root selector, property access with dot or bracket notation, array wildcards, and array indexing.

Now I'm thinking through what to actually export—the constraint itself gets used through the `gen()` function's parameters, so I need to add `json_path` as a parameter there and export the conversion utility function from the main module for users who want to work with it programmatically.

Let me outline the files I need to create and modify: a new constraint module with the path-to-schema converter and a validator, plus updates to the generator and main exports. I need to add the `_handle_json_path_to_schema()` method to the runtime endpoint to convert JSONPath to JSON schema during generation, update the interpreter to recognize the json_path parameter, and export the conversion utility from the main package. Let me also reconsider how this integrates with the SRT backend—the flow should convert json_path to json_schema at the runtime level before passing to the backend.

Now I'm designing the JSONPath parser...

Writing the conversion function... I'm defining a function to convert JSONPath expressions into JSON Schema format, which validates that specific paths exist in the generated JSON output. The module also includes a validation function to check whether text conforms to a given JSONPath constraint.

Parsing JSONPath expressions... Still parsing the path expression... Building schema from path steps... I'm realizing I need to reconsider how the steps are ordered and structured. For a path like `$.store.book[*].author`, the steps should follow the actual traversal order: first the `store` property, then the `book` array with a wildcard, then the `author` property. The schema construction needs to properly nest based on this ordering, ensuring that array items conform to the remaining path constraints. Building the schema from reversed steps...

Since my path parser returns steps in original order (store → book → author), reversing them lets me construct from the innermost schema outward—starting with the author property, then wrapping it in the book array structure, and finally nesting that under the store property.

Now I need to check what parameters `_resolve_sampling_params` expects and find where to add `json_path` support throughout the interpreter.

def _resolve_sampling_params(self, sampling_params):
        """
        Construct sampling param based on default + override values

        The default values of sampling are populated in `default_sampling_para` via sgl.function.run(...sampling_args)
        , and `sampling_params` contains the override values from sgl.gen().

        Here we use default_sampling_para as the base and override the values if they exist in `sampling_params`.
        It also extends the stop tokens based on the chat template.
        """

        # deepcopy is required because the dict has lists inside
        clone = copy.deepcopy(self.default_sampling_para)

        for item in [
            "max_new_tokens",
            "min_new_tokens",
            "n",
            "stop",
            "stop_token_ids",
            "stop_regex",
            "temperature",
            "top_p",
            "top_k",
            "min_p",
            "frequency_penalty",
            "presence_penalty",
            "ignore_eos",
            "return_logprob",
            "logprob_start_len",
            "top_logprobs_num",
            "return_text_in_logprobs",
            "dtype",
            "regex",
            "json_schema",
        ]:
            value = getattr(sampling_params, item, None)
            if value is not None:
                setattr(clone, item, value)

        if self.chat_template.stop_str:
            if clone.stop == ():
                clone.stop = []
            elif isinstance(clone.stop, str):
                clone.stop = [clone.stop]
            clone.stop += self.chat_template.stop_str

        return clone

    def __del__(self):
        self.end()


class ProgramState:
    """The state of an SGL program."""

    def __init__(self, stream_executor: StreamExecutor):
        self.stream_executor = stream_executor

    def _role_common(self, name: str, expr: Optional[SglExpr] = None):

input.content:
# Plan: Add `json_path` Output Constraint to SGLang

## Context

SGLang supports constrained generation via `regex`, `json_schema`, and `ebnf` parameters in `gen()`. These flow from the frontend API through an IR layer to a runtime backend (xgrammar/outlines/llguidance) that enforces token-level constraints during generation.

This plan adds a new `json_path` constraint that accepts a JSONPath expression (e.g., `"$.store.book[*].author"`) and ensures the generated output is valid JSON containing the specified path. The implementation converts the JSONPath to a JSON schema at the frontend layer—consistent with how `dtype` is converted to a regex—so the existing backend grammar engines can enforce the constraint during generation with no backend changes needed.

---

## Architecture Overview

```
gen(json_path="$.store.book[*].author")
    ↓
SglGen / SglSamplingParams.json_path
    ↓
_resolve_sampling_params() propagates json_path
    ↓
RuntimeEndpoint._handle_json_path_to_schema()
  → calls json_path_to_json_schema() → produces JSON schema string
  → sets sampling_params.json_schema = derived_schema
    ↓
sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs() sends json_schema to SRT backend
    ↓
Existing grammar backend (xgrammar/outlines/llguidance) enforces it
```

This mirrors exactly the `dtype → regex` conversion pattern already in the codebase.

---

## Files to Create

### 1. `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path_constraint.py` (NEW)

Core logic module. Contains:

**`json_path_to_json_schema(json_path: str) -> str`**
- Parses the JSONPath expression with `_parse_json_path()`
- Builds a nested JSON schema dict with `_build_schema_from_steps()`
- Returns `json.dumps(schema)`
- Raises `ValueError` for unsupported constructs (deep scan `..`, bare bracket index at root)

**`validate_json_path(text: str, json_path: str) -> bool`**
- Post-generation validator (useful for testing and non-SRT backends)
- Parses `text` as JSON, returns `False` on `JSONDecodeError`
- Walks the path steps via `_check_path_exists()`

**Internal `_parse_json_path(json_path: str) -> List[Step]`**
- Strips `$` prefix, iterates over remaining string
- Returns a list of typed steps (in path order):
  - `("property", "name")` — for `.name` and `['name']` notation
  - `("array_wildcard", "name")` — for `.name[*]`
  - `("array_index", "name", int)` — for `.name[0]`
- Raises `ValueError` for unsupported syntax (e.g., `..` deep scan)

**Internal `_build_schema_from_steps(steps: List[Step]) -> dict`**
- Iterates steps in **reverse** to build schema inside-out
- For `("property", name)`:
  ```python
  {"type": "object", "properties": {name: inner}, "required": [name]}
  ```
- For `("array_wildcard", name)`:
  ```python
  {"type": "object", "properties": {name: {"type": "array", "items": inner, "minItems": 1}}, "required": [name]}
  ```
- For `("array_index", name, idx)`:
  ```python
  {"type": "object", "properties": {name: {"type": "array", "minItems": idx+1, "items": inner}}, "required": [name]}
  ```
- Inner-most schema starts as `{}` (any value)

**Internal `_check_path_exists(data: Any, steps: List[Step]) -> bool`**
- Recursively walks `data` following `steps`
- Returns `True` only if all steps resolve successfully
- For `array_wildcard`: all items in the array must satisfy the remaining path

**Supported JSONPath subset:**
- `$` — root
- `.property` — dot-notation property
- `['property']` — bracket-notation property
- `[*]` — array wildcard (when appended to a property step)
- `[N]` — array index (when appended to a property step)

---

## Files to Modify

### 2. `python/sglang/lang/ir.py`

**In `SglSamplingParams` dataclass** — add field after `json_schema`:
```python
json_schema: Optional[str] = None

# for constrained generation (json_path is converted to json_schema at frontend)
dtype: Optional[str] = None
regex: Optional[str] = None
json_path: Optional[str] = None   # ← ADD THIS
```

`clone()` — no change needed (matches existing pattern: `dtype` and `regex` are also excluded from `clone()` and applied via `_resolve_sampling_params` overrides).

**In `SglGen.__init__()`** — add parameter and pass to `SglSamplingParams`:
```python
def __init__(
    self,
    ...
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
    json_path: Optional[str] = None,   # ← ADD THIS
):
    ...
    self.sampling_params = SglSamplingParams(
        ...
        regex=regex,
        json_schema=json_schema,
        json_path=json_path,           # ← ADD THIS
    )
```

### 3. `python/sglang/lang/api.py`

**In `gen()`** — add parameter with basic validation:
```python
def gen(
    ...
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
    json_path: Optional[str] = None,   # ← ADD THIS
):
    ...
    # validate json_path format
    if json_path is not None:
        if not json_path.startswith("$"):
            raise ValueError(f"json_path must start with '$', got: {json_path!r}")

    return SglGen(
        name,
```

# Plan: Add `json_path` Output Constraint to SGLang

## Context

SGLang supports constrained generation via `regex`, `json_schema`, and `ebnf` parameters in `gen()`. These flow from the frontend API through an IR layer to a runtime backend (xgrammar/outlines/llguidance) that enforces token-level constraints during generation.

This plan adds a new `json_path` constraint that accepts a JSONPath expression (e.g., `"$.store.book[*].author"`) and ensures the generated output is valid JSON containing the specified path. The implementation converts the JSONPath to a JSON schema at the frontend layer—consistent with how `dtype` is converted to a regex—so the existing backend grammar engines can enforce the constraint during generation with no backend changes needed.

---

## Architecture Overview

```
gen(json_path="$.store.book[*].author")
    ↓
SglGen / SglSamplingParams.json_path
    ↓
_resolve_sampling_params() propagates json_path
    ↓
RuntimeEndpoint._handle_json_path_to_schema()
  → calls json_path_to_json_schema() → produces JSON schema string
  → sets sampling_params.json_schema = derived_schema
    ↓
sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs() sends json_schema to SRT backend
    ↓
Existing grammar backend (xgrammar/outlines/llguidance) enforces it
```

This mirrors exactly the `dtype → regex` conversion pattern already in the codebase.

---

## Files to Create

### 1. `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path_constraint.py` (NEW)

Core logic module. Contains:

**`json_path_to_json_schema(json_path: str) -> str`**
- Parses the JSONPath expression with `_parse_json_path()`
- Builds a nested JSON schema dict with `_build_schema_from_steps()`
- Returns `json.dumps(schema)`
- Raises `ValueError` for unsupported constructs (deep scan `..`, bare bracket index at root)

**`validate_json_path(text: str, json_path: str) -> bool`**
- Post-generation validator (useful for testing and non-SRT backends)
- Parses `text` as JSON, returns `False` on `JSONDecodeError`
- Walks the path steps via `_check_path_exists()`

**Internal `_parse_json_path(json_path: str) -> List[Step]`**
- Strips `$` prefix, iterates over remaining string
- Returns a list of typed steps (in path order):
  - `("property", "name")` — for `.name` and `['name']` notation
  - `("array_wildcard", "name")` — for `.name[*]`
  - `("array_index", "name", int)` — for `.name[0]`
- Raises `ValueError` for unsupported syntax (e.g., `..` deep scan)

**Internal `_build_schema_from_steps(steps: List[Step]) -> dict`**
- Iterates steps in **reverse** to build schema inside-out
- For `("property", name)`:
  ```python
  {"type": "object", "properties": {name: inner}, "required": [name]}
  ```
- For `("array_wildcard", name)`:
  ```python
  {"type": "object", "properties": {name: {"type": "array", "items": inner, "minItems": 1}}, "required": [name]}
  ```
- For `("array_index", name, idx)`:
  ```python
  {"type": "object", "properties": {name: {"type": "array", "minItems": idx+1, "items": inner}}, "required": [name]}
  ```
- Inner-most schema starts as `{}` (any value)

**Internal `_check_path_exists(data: Any, steps: List[Step]) -> bool`**
- Recursively walks `data` following `steps`
- Returns `True` only if all steps resolve successfully
- For `array_wildcard`: all items in the array must satisfy the remaining path

**Supported JSONPath subset:**
- `$` — root
- `.property` — dot-notation property
- `['property']` — bracket-notation property
- `[*]` — array wildcard (when appended to a property step)
- `[N]` — array index (when appended to a property step)

---

## Files to Modify

### 2. `python/sglang/lang/ir.py`

**In `SglSamplingParams` dataclass** — add field after `json_schema`:
```python
json_schema: Optional[str] = None

# for constrained generation (json_path is converted to json_schema at frontend)
dtype: Optional[str] = None
regex: Optional[str] = None
json_path: Optional[str] = None   # ← ADD THIS
```

`clone()` — no change needed (matches existing pattern: `dtype` and `regex` are also excluded from `clone()` and applied via `_resolve_sampling_params` overrides).

**In `SglGen.__init__()`** — add parameter and pass to `SglSamplingParams`:
```python
def __init__(
    self,
    ...
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
    json_path: Optional[str] = None,   # ← ADD THIS
):
    ...
    self.sampling_params = SglSamplingParams(
        ...
        regex=regex,
        json_schema=json_schema,
        json_path=json_path,           # ← ADD THIS
    )
```

### 3. `python/sglang/lang/api.py`

**In `gen()`** — add parameter with basic validation:
```python
def gen(
    ...
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
    json_path: Optional[str] = None,   # ← ADD THIS
):
    ...
    # validate json_path format
    if json_path is not None:
        if not json_path.startswith("$"):
            raise ValueError(f"json_path must start with '$', got: {json_path!r}")

    return SglGen(
        name,
        ...
        regex,
        json_schema,
        json_path,                     # ← ADD THIS
    )
```

### 4. `python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py`

**In `_resolve_sampling_params()`** — add `"json_path"` to the override list:
```python
for item in [
    "max_new_tokens", "min_new_tokens", "n", "stop", "stop_token_ids",
    "stop_regex", "temperature", "top_p", "top_k", "min_p",
    "frequency_penalty", "presence_penalty", "ignore_eos",
    "return_logprob", "logprob_start_len", "top_logprobs_num",
    "return_text_in_logprobs", "dtype", "regex", "json_schema",
    "json_path",   # ← ADD THIS
]:
```

### 5. `python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py`

**Add `_handle_json_path_to_schema()` method:**
```python
def _handle_json_path_to_schema(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
    """Convert json_path to a json_schema for constrained generation."""
    if sampling_params.json_path is None:
        return
    from sglang.srt.constrained.json_path_constraint import json_path_to_json_schema
    if sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
        warnings.warn(
            "Both json_path and json_schema are set. json_path will be converted "
            "to a JSON schema, overriding the explicit json_schema."
        )
    sampling_params.json_schema = json_path_to_json_schema(sampling_params.json_path)
```

**In `generate()` and `generate_stream()`** — call the new method immediately after `_handle_dtype_to_regex`:
```python

self._handle_json_path_to_schema(sampling_params)   # ← ADD THIS
```

### 6. `python/sglang/__init__.py`

Add import and export of the public utilities:
```python

    json_path_to_json_schema,
    validate_json_path,
)
```

Add to `__all__`:
```python
"json_path_to_json_schema",
"validate_json_path",
```

---

## Example Usage (after implementation)

SGLang has three existing constrained generation types: `regex`, `json_schema`, and `ebnf`. They all flow through:

5. **SRT backend grammar engines** (xgrammar/outlines/llguidance) → enforce token-level constraints

The key insight: `json_path` can be converted to a **JSON schema** at the frontend layer, exactly like `dtype` is converted to a `regex`. This requires **no changes to the SRT backend grammar engines**.

---

`$.store.book[*].author` is converted to this schema (built inside-out from the path):

tool_input.plan:
# Plan: Add `json_path` Output Constraint to SGLang

## Context

SGLang supports constrained generation via `regex`, `json_schema`, and `ebnf` parameters in `gen()`. These flow from the frontend API through an IR layer to a runtime backend (xgrammar/outlines/llguidance) that enforces token-level constraints during generation.

This plan adds a new `json_path` constraint that accepts a JSONPath expression (e.g., `"$.store.book[*].author"`) and ensures the generated output is valid JSON containing the specified path. The implementation converts the JSONPath to a JSON schema at the frontend layer—consistent with how `dtype` is converted to a regex—so the existing backend grammar engines can enforce the constraint during generation with no backend changes needed.

---

## Architecture Overview

```
gen(json_path="$.store.book[*].author")
    ↓
SglGen / SglSamplingParams.json_path
    ↓
_resolve_sampling_params() propagates json_path
    ↓
RuntimeEndpoint._handle_json_path_to_schema()
  → calls json_path_to_json_schema() → produces JSON schema string
  → sets sampling_params.json_schema = derived_schema
    ↓
sampling_params.to_srt_kwargs() sends json_schema to SRT backend
    ↓
Existing grammar backend (xgrammar/outlines/llguidance) enforces it
```

This mirrors exactly the `dtype → regex` conversion pattern already in the codebase.

---

## Files to Create

### 1. `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path_constraint.py` (NEW)

Core logic module. Contains:

**`json_path_to_json_schema(json_path: str) -> str`**
- Parses the JSONPath expression with `_parse_json_path()`
- Builds a nested JSON schema dict with `_build_schema_from_steps()`
- Returns `json.dumps(schema)`
- Raises `ValueError` for unsupported constructs (deep scan `..`, bare bracket index at root)

**`validate_json_path(text: str, json_path: str) -> bool`**
- Post-generation validator (useful for testing and non-SRT backends)
- Parses `text` as JSON, returns `False` on `JSONDecodeError`
- Walks the path steps via `_check_path_exists()`

**Internal `_parse_json_path(json_path: str) -> List[Step]`**
- Strips `$` prefix, iterates over remaining string
- Returns a list of typed steps (in path order):
  - `("property", "name")` — for `.name` and `['name']` notation
  - `("array_wildcard", "name")` — for `.name[*]`
  - `("array_index", "name", int)` — for `.name[0]`
- Raises `ValueError` for unsupported syntax (e.g., `..` deep scan)

**Internal `_build_schema_from_steps(steps: List[Step]) -> dict`**
- Iterates steps in **reverse** to build schema inside-out
- For `("property", name)`:
  ```python
  {"type": "object", "properties": {name: inner}, "required": [name]}
  ```
- For `("array_wildcard", name)`:
  ```python
  {"type": "object", "properties": {name: {"type": "array", "items": inner, "minItems": 1}}, "required": [name]}
  ```
- For `("array_index", name, idx)`:
  ```python
  {"type": "object", "properties": {name: {"type": "array", "minItems": idx+1, "items": inner}}, "required": [name]}
  ```
- Inner-most schema starts as `{}` (any value)

**Internal `_check_path_exists(data: Any, steps: List[Step]) -> bool`**
- Recursively walks `data` following `steps`
- Returns `True` only if all steps resolve successfully
- For `array_wildcard`: all items in the array must satisfy the remaining path

**Supported JSONPath subset:**
- `$` — root
- `.property` — dot-notation property
- `['property']` — bracket-notation property
- `[*]` — array wildcard (when appended to a property step)
- `[N]` — array index (when appended to a property step)

---

## Files to Modify

### 2. `python/sglang/lang/ir.py`

**In `SglSamplingParams` dataclass** — add field after `json_schema`:
```python
json_schema: Optional[str] = None

# for constrained generation (json_path is converted to json_schema at frontend)
dtype: Optional[str] = None
regex: Optional[str] = None
json_path: Optional[str] = None   # ← ADD THIS
```

`clone()` — no change needed (matches existing pattern: `dtype` and `regex` are also excluded from `clone()` and applied via `_resolve_sampling_params` overrides).

**In `SglGen.__init__()`** — add parameter and pass to `SglSamplingParams`:
```python
def __init__(
    self,
    ...
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
    json_path: Optional[str] = None,   # ← ADD THIS
):
    ...
    self.sampling_params = SglSamplingParams(
        ...
        regex=regex,
        json_schema=json_schema,
        json_path=json_path,           # ← ADD THIS
    )
```

### 3. `python/sglang/lang/api.py`

**In `gen()`** — add parameter with basic validation:
```python
def gen(
    ...
    regex: Optional[str] = None,
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
    json_path: Optional[str] = None,   # ← ADD THIS
):
    ...
    # validate json_path format
    if json_path is not None:
        if not json_path.startswith("$"):
            raise ValueError(f"json_path must start with '$', got: {json_path!r}")

    return SglGen(
        name,
        ...
        regex,
        json_schema,
        json_path,                     # ← ADD THIS
    )
```

### 4. `python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py`

**In `_resolve_sampling_params()`** — add `"json_path"` to the override list:
```python
for item in [
    "max_new_tokens", "min_new_tokens", "n", "stop", "stop_token_ids",
    "stop_regex", "temperature", "top_p", "top_k", "min_p",
    "frequency_penalty", "presence_penalty", "ignore_eos",
    "return_logprob", "logprob_start_len", "top_logprobs_num",
    "return_text_in_logprobs", "dtype", "regex", "json_schema",
    "json_path",   # ← ADD THIS
]:
```

### 5. `python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py`

**Add `_handle_json_path_to_schema()` method:**
```python
def _handle_json_path_to_schema(self, sampling_params: SglSamplingParams):
    """Convert json_path to a json_schema for constrained generation."""
    if sampling_params.json_path is None:
        return
    from sglang.srt.constrained.json_path_constraint import json_path_to_json_schema
    if sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
        warnings.warn(
            "Both json_path and json_schema are set. json_path will be converted "
            "to a JSON schema, overriding the explicit json_schema."
        )
    sampling_params.json_schema = json_path_to_json_schema(sampling_params.json_path)
```

**In `generate()` and `generate_stream()`** — call the new method immediately after `_handle_dtype_to_regex`:
```python
self._handle_dtype_to_regex(sampling_params)
self._handle_json_path_to_schema(sampling_params)   # ← ADD THIS
```

### 6. `python/sglang/__init__.py`

Add import and export of the public utilities:
```python
from sglang.srt.constrained.json_path_constraint import (
    json_path_to_json_schema,
    validate_json_path,
)
```

Add to `__all__`:
```python
"json_path_to_json_schema",
"validate_json_path",
```

---

## Example Usage (after implementation)

```python
import sglang as sgl

@sgl.function
def extract_authors(s):
    s += "List books in JSON format:\n"
    s += sgl.gen(
        "books",
        max_tokens=256,
        json_path="$.store.book[*].author",
    )

state = extract_authors.run()
print(state["books"])
# Output is guaranteed to be JSON matching {"store": {"book": [{"author": ...}, ...]}}
```

Programmatic use of the converter:
```python
from sglang import json_path_to_json_schema, validate_json_path

schema = json_path_to_json_schema("$.name")
# → '{"type": "object", "properties": {"name": {}}, "required": ["name"]}'

ok = validate_json_path('{"name": "Alice"}', "$.name")   # True
ok = validate_json_path('{"age": 30}', "$.name")          # False
```

---

## Verification

Since the implementation cannot be run, verify correctness by:

1. **Unit logic check** — trace `json_path_to_json_schema("$.store.book[*].author")` manually:
   - `_parse_json_path` → `[("property","store"), ("array_wildcard","book"), ("property","author")]`
   - `_build_schema_from_steps` reversed → wraps `{}` → `{..author..}` → `{..book array..}` → `{..store..}`
   - Final schema has `required: [store]`, `store.required: [book]`, `book.type: array`, `book.items.required: [author]`

2. **Validation check** — test `validate_json_path`:
   - Valid: `'{"store": {"book": [{"author": "Bob"}]}}'` with `"$.store.book[*].author"` → `True`
   - Invalid: `'{"store": {}}'` with same path → `False`
   - Invalid JSON: `'not json'` → `False`

3. **Integration check** — verify the schema produced by `json_path_to_json_schema` is valid JSON Schema 
   parseable by `json.loads()` and compatible with xgrammar/outlines backends (both accept standard draft-7 schemas).

4. **Error check** — verify `ValueError` is raised for:
   - Path not starting with `$`
   - Deep scan `..`
   - Bare `[*]` at root without a preceding property

---

## Critical Files (summary)

| File | Action | Purpose |
|------|--------|---------|
| `python/sglang/srt/constrained/json_path_constraint.py` | **CREATE** | Core converter and validator |
| `python/sglang/lang/ir.py` | modify | Add `json_path` field to `SglSamplingParams` and `SglGen` |
| `python/sglang/lang/api.py` | modify | Add `json_path` param to `gen()` with basic validation |
| `python/sglang/lang/interpreter.py` | modify | Propagate `json_path` in `_resolve_sampling_params()` |
| `python/sglang/lang/backend/runtime_endpoint.py` | modify | Add conversion method + call it in `generate()` / `generate_stream()` |
| `python/sglang/__init__.py` | modify | Export public utility functions |

1	from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
2	from dataclasses import dataclass
3	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
4	
5	import numpy as np
6	
7	
8	@dataclass
9	class ChoicesDecision:
10	    decision: str
11	    meta_info: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
12	
13	
14	class ChoicesSamplingMethod(ABC):
15	
16	    @property
17	    def requires_unconditional_logprobs(self) -> bool:
18	        return False
19	
20	    @abstractmethod
21	    def __call__(
22	        self,
23	        *,
24	        choices: List[str],
25	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
26	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
27	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
28	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
29	    ) -> ChoicesDecision: ...
30	
31	
32	class TokenLengthNormalized(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
33	
34	    def __call__(
35	        self,
36	        *,
37	        choices: List[str],
38	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
39	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
40	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
41	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
42	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
43	        """Select the option with the highest token length normalized prompt logprob."""
44	        best_choice = choices[np.argmax(normalized_prompt_logprobs)]
45	        meta_info = {
46	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
47	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
48	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
49	        }
50	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
51	
52	
53	token_length_normalized = TokenLengthNormalized()
54	
55	
56	class GreedyTokenSelection(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
57	
58	    def __call__(
59	        self,
60	        *,
61	        choices: List[str],
62	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
63	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
64	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
65	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
66	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
67	        """Select the option based on greedy logprob selection. For overlapping options
68	        where one option is a subset of a longer option, extend the shorter option using
69	        its average logprob for comparison against the longer option."""
70	
71	        num_options = len(choices)
72	        max_tokens = max(len(option) for option in input_token_logprobs)
73	        logprob_matrix = self._build_logprob_matrix(
74	            input_token_logprobs, max_tokens, num_options
75	        )
76	        remaining = self._greedy_selection(logprob_matrix, num_options, max_tokens)
77	
78	        best_choice = choices[remaining[0]]
79	        meta_info = {
80	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
81	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
82	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
83	            "greedy_logprob_matrix": logprob_matrix.tolist(),
84	        }
85	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
86	
87	    def _build_logprob_matrix(self, input_token_logprobs, max_tokens, num_options):
88	        logprob_matrix = np.zeros((num_options, max_tokens))
89	        for i, option in enumerate(input_token_logprobs):
90	            actual_logprobs = [token[0] for token in option]
91	            avg_logprob = np.mean(actual_logprobs)
92	            logprob_matrix[i, : len(option)] = actual_logprobs
93	            if len(option) < max_tokens:
94	                logprob_matrix[i, len(option) :] = avg_logprob
95	        return logprob_matrix
96	
97	    def _greedy_selection(self, logprob_matrix, num_options, max_tokens):
98	        remaining = np.arange(num_options)
99	        for j in range(max_tokens):
100	            max_logprob = np.max(logprob_matrix[remaining, j])
101	            remaining = remaining[logprob_matrix[remaining, j] == max_logprob]
102	            if len(remaining) == 1:
103	                break
104	        return remaining
105	
106	
107	greedy_token_selection = GreedyTokenSelection()
108	
109	
110	class UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
111	
112	    @property
113	    def requires_unconditional_logprobs(self) -> bool:
114	        return True
115	
116	    def __call__(
117	        self,
118	        *,
119	        choices: List[str],
120	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
121	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
122	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
123	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
124	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
125	        """Select the option with the highest average token logprob once normalized by
126	        the unconditional token logprobs.
127	
128	        The first unconditional token logprob is assumed to be None. If so, it is
129	        replaced with 0 for the purposes of normalization."""
130	
131	        if unconditional_token_logprobs is None:
132	            raise ValueError(
133	                "Unconditional token logprobs are required for this method."
134	            )
135	
136	        normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs = self._normalize_logprobs(
137	            input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs
138	        )
139	
140	        best_choice = choices[np.argmax(normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs)]
141	        meta_info = {
142	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
143	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
144	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
145	            "unconditional_token_logprobs": unconditional_token_logprobs,
146	            "normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs": normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs,
147	        }
148	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
149	
150	    def _normalize_logprobs(self, input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs):
151	        normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs = []
152	        for inputs, unconditionals in zip(
153	            input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs
154	        ):
155	            inputs_logprobs = np.array([token[0] for token in inputs])
156	            unconditionals_logprobs = np.array([token[0] for token in unconditionals])
157	            unconditionals_logprobs[0] = unconditionals_logprobs[0] or 0
158	            normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs.append(
159	                float(np.mean(inputs_logprobs - unconditionals_logprobs))
160	            )
161	        return normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs
162	
163	
164	unconditional_likelihood_normalized = UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized()
165

3	import dataclasses
4	import inspect
5	import warnings
6	from typing import List, Optional, Union
7	
8	from sglang.global_config import global_config
9	from sglang.lang.choices import ChoicesSamplingMethod
10	
11	REGEX_INT = r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
12	REGEX_FLOAT = r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
13	REGEX_BOOL = r"(True|False)"
14	REGEX_STR = r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""  # bugs with regex r"\".*\"" in interegular pkg
15	
16	
17	@dataclasses.dataclass
18	class SglSamplingParams:
19	    max_new_tokens: int = 128
20	    min_new_tokens: int = 0
21	    n: int = 1
22	    stop: Union[str, List[str]] = ()
23	    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = ()
24	    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = ()
25	    temperature: float = 1.0
26	    top_p: float = 1.0
27	    top_k: int = -1  # -1 means disable
28	    min_p: float = 0.0
29	    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
30	    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
31	    ignore_eos: bool = False
32	    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None
33	    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = (None,)
34	    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = (None,)
35	    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = (None,)
36	    json_schema: Optional[str] = None
37	
38	    # for constrained generation, not included in to_xxx_kwargs
39	    dtype: Optional[str] = None
40	    regex: Optional[str] = None
41	
42	    def clone(self):
43	        return SglSamplingParams(
44	            self.max_new_tokens,
45	            self.min_new_tokens,
46	            self.n,
47	            self.stop,
48	            self.stop_token_ids,
49	            self.stop_regex,
50	            self.temperature,
51	            self.top_p,
52	            self.top_k,
53	            self.min_p,
54	            self.frequency_penalty,
55	            self.presence_penalty,
56	            self.ignore_eos,
57	            self.return_logprob,
58	            self.logprob_start_len,
59	            self.top_logprobs_num,
60	            self.return_text_in_logprobs,
61	            self.json_schema,
62	        )
63	
64	    def to_openai_kwargs(self):
65	        # OpenAI does not support top_k, so we drop it here
66	        if self.regex is not None:
67	            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the OpenAI backend.")
68	        return {
69	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
70	            "max_completion_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
71	            "n": self.n,
72	            "stop": self.stop or None,
73	            "temperature": self.temperature,
74	            "top_p": self.top_p,
75	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
76	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
77	        }
78	
79	    def to_vertexai_kwargs(self):
80	        if self.regex is not None:
81	            warnings.warn(
82	                "Regular expression is not supported in the VertexAI backend."
83	            )
84	        return {
85	            "candidate_count": 1,
86	            "max_output_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
87	            "stop_sequences": self.stop,
88	            "temperature": self.temperature,
89	            "top_p": self.top_p,
90	            "top_k": self.top_k if self.top_k > 0 else None,
91	        }
92	
93	    def to_anthropic_kwargs(self):
94	        # Anthropic does not support frequency_penalty or presence_penalty, so we drop it here
95	        if self.regex is not None:
96	            warnings.warn(
97	                "Regular expression is not supported in the Anthropic backend."
98	            )
99	        return {
100	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
101	            "stop_sequences": (
102	                self.stop if isinstance(self.stop, (list, tuple)) else [self.stop]
103	            ),
104	            "temperature": self.temperature,
105	            "top_p": self.top_p,
106	            "top_k": self.top_k,
107	        }
108	
109	    def to_litellm_kwargs(self):
110	        if self.regex is not None:
111	            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the LiteLLM backend.")
112	        return {
113	            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
114	            "stop": self.stop or None,
115	            "temperature": self.temperature,
116	            "top_p": self.top_p,
117	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
118	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
119	        }
120	
121	    def to_srt_kwargs(self):
122	        return {
123	            "max_new_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
124	            "min_new_tokens": self.min_new_tokens,
125	            "n": self.n,
126	            "stop": self.stop,
127	            "stop_token_ids": self.stop_token_ids,
128	            "stop_regex": self.stop_regex,
129	            "temperature": self.temperature,
130	            "top_p": self.top_p,
131	            "top_k": self.top_k,
132	            "min_p": self.min_p,
133	            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
134	            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
135	            "ignore_eos": self.ignore_eos,
136	            "regex": self.regex,
137	            "json_schema": self.json_schema,
138	        }

141	class SglFunction:
142	    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
143	        self.func = func
144	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
145	        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
146	        self.pin_prefix_rid = None
147	
148	        # Parse arguments
149	        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
150	        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'
151	        self.arg_names = argspec.args[1:]
152	        self.arg_defaults = argspec.defaults if argspec.defaults is not None else []
153	
154	    def bind(self, **kwargs):
155	        assert all(key in self.arg_names for key in kwargs)
156	
157	        new_bind_dict = {**self.bind_arguments, **kwargs}
158	        return SglFunction(self.func, bind_arguments=new_bind_dict)
159	
160	    def run(
161	        self,
162	        *args,
163	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
164	        n: int = 1,
165	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
166	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
167	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
168	        temperature: float = 1.0,
169	        top_p: float = 1.0,
170	        top_k: int = -1,
171	        min_p: float = 0.0,
172	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
173	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
174	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
175	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
176	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
177	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
178	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
179	        stream: bool = False,
180	        backend=None,
181	        use_thread: bool = True,
182	        **kwargs,
183	    ):
184	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program
185	
186	        # avoid using [] as the default arg: https://nikos7am.com/posts/mutable-default-arguments/
187	        if stop is None:
188	            stop = []
189	        if stop_token_ids is None:
190	            stop_token_ids = []
191	        if stop_regex is None:
192	            stop_regex = []
193	
194	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
195	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
196	            n=n,
197	            stop=stop,
198	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
199	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
200	            temperature=temperature,
201	            top_p=top_p,
202	            top_k=top_k,
203	            min_p=min_p,
204	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
205	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
206	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
207	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
208	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
209	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
210	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
211	        )
212	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
213	        return run_program(
214	            self,
215	            backend,
216	            args,
217	            kwargs,
218	            default_sampling_para,
219	            stream,
220	            use_thread=use_thread,
221	        )
222	
223	    def run_batch(
224	        self,
225	        batch_kwargs,
226	        *,
227	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
228	        n: int = 1,
229	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
230	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
231	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
232	        temperature: float = 1.0,
233	        top_p: float = 1.0,
234	        top_k: int = -1,
235	        min_p: float = 0.0,
236	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
237	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
238	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
239	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
240	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
241	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
242	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
243	        backend=None,
244	        num_threads: Union[str, int] = "auto",
245	        progress_bar: bool = False,
246	        generator_style: bool = False,
247	    ):
248	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program_batch
249	
250	        if stop is None:
251	            stop = []
252	        if stop_token_ids is None:
253	            stop_token_ids = []
254	        if stop_regex is None:
255	            stop_regex = []
256	
257	        assert isinstance(batch_kwargs, (list, tuple))
258	        if len(batch_kwargs) == 0:
259	            return []
260	        if not isinstance(batch_kwargs[0], dict):
261	            num_programs = len(batch_kwargs)
262	            # change the list of argument values to dict of arg_name -> arg_value
263	            batch_kwargs = [
264	                {self.arg_names[i]: v for i, v in enumerate(arg_values)}
265	                for arg_values in batch_kwargs
266	                if isinstance(arg_values, (list, tuple))
267	                and len(self.arg_names) - len(self.arg_defaults)
268	                <= len(arg_values)
269	                <= len(self.arg_names)
270	            ]
271	            # Ensure to raise an exception if the number of arguments mismatch
272	            if len(batch_kwargs) != num_programs:
273	                raise Exception("Given arguments mismatch the SGL function signature")
274	
275	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
276	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
277	            n=n,
278	            stop=stop,
279	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
280	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
281	            temperature=temperature,
282	            top_p=top_p,
283	            top_k=top_k,
284	            min_p=min_p,
285	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
286	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
287	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
288	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
289	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
290	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
291	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
292	        )
293	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
294	        return run_program_batch(
295	            self,
296	            backend,
297	            batch_kwargs,
298	            default_sampling_para,
299	            num_threads,
300	            progress_bar,
301	            generator_style=generator_style,
302	        )
303	
304	    def trace(self, *, backend=None, **kwargs):
305	        from sglang.lang.tracer import trace_program
306	
307	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
308	        return trace_program(self, kwargs, backend)
309	
310	    def cache(self, backend=None):
311	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import cache_program
312	
313	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
314	        return cache_program(self, backend)
315	
316	    def __call__(self, *args, **kwargs):
317	        from sglang.lang.tracer import TracingScope
318	
319	        tracing_scope = TracingScope.get_current_scope()
320	        if tracing_scope is None:
321	            return self.run(*args, **kwargs)
322	        else:
323	            kwargs["backend"] = tracing_scope.tracer_state.backend
324	            return self.trace(*args, **kwargs)
